location.office_time_required: 3 days per week on Mondays, Wednesdays, and Thursdays

level_signals: 5+ years in BizOps, Finance Ops, Product Ops, or Consulting
Comp range $132,000–$165,000 base in SF, consistent with senior IC or manager-level
Executive presence and stakeholder leadership responsibilities implied

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 5+ years in BizOps, Finance Ops, Product Ops, or Consulting
Direct experience in M&A integration, large-scale program management, or product launches
Strong understanding of Finance functions (Accounting, FP&A, Treasury) and financial workflows (QTC, rev rec, billing)
Ability to operate in ambiguity and drive clarity across senior stakeholders
Proven track record of driving execution across cross-functional teams, across Finance, Business, and Technical teams
Strong systems thinking—understanding how processes, systems, and people connect

scope.surface: Finance Business Operations — M&A integration, new product initiatives (NPIs), and strategic programs (AVs, Luxury)
scope.business_model: marketplace / rideshare platform; B2C and B2B
primary_metrics_inferred: Finance integration completeness and speed post-M&A close
NPI financial readiness and clean launch execution
Reduction of operational and technical debt
core_responsibilities: Act as the central Finance lead for new product initiatives and large strategic programs
Support end-to-end Finance integration across global acquisitions, from Finance integration strategy during diligence to post-close integration
Drive execution across multiple complex, cross-functional programs simultaneously
Build and manage integrated plans, milestones, and KPIs
Define criteria (impact, risk, effort, strategic alignment)
Help stand up and evolve the Finance bizops function and define best practices and processes
Codify repeatable finance integration playbooks to improve speed, quality, and predictability

compensation.equity_form: potential equity offering mentioned but not detailed

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed ($132K–$165K base in SF)
Clearly scoped role with defined ownership areas (M&A integration, NPIs, strategic programs)
Role sits at center of high-visibility, high-impact work (M&A, AVs, Luxury)
Hybrid schedule with 4 weeks/year remote flexibility
Comprehensive benefits including 18 weeks parental leave and 401(k) match
red_flags: In-office 3 days per week required (Mon/Wed/Thu) — rigid schedule may deter some candidates
Role is new/being built out — 'stand up and evolve the Finance bizops function' implies limited existing structure or support
Visa sponsorship not addressed in JD
Geographic pay differential mentioned but only SF range disclosed

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range and responsibilities; not explicitly stated)

ambiguities: JD lists multiple OR conditions for experience qualification ('M&A integration, large-scale program management, or product launches') — any one may suffice, but M&A integration is most emphasized throughout the body.
Finance functions knowledge requirement includes 'BS in Accounting/Finance preferred' as a subordinate clause — treated as nice-to-have (soft) rather than hard requirement given 'preferred' language.
Role title says 'Manager' but no direct reports are mentioned; may be an IC-level role with management scope over programs rather than people.
